html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Twenty-One

The Book of Ruth

RUTH

Covenant History — The Story of Covenant Loyalty

Chapters 1 through 4

Where You Go I Will Go — Your People Shall Be My People — Spread Your Wings Over Your Servant — A Son Has Been Born to Na'omi — The Genealogy of Dawid

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

Orit Ge'ez — Ethiopian Complete Canon

Masoretic Text

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

RUTH — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 4 — COVENANT LOYALTY AND THE ANCESTRESS OF DAWID

RUTH — CHAPTER 1 A Man of Beit-Lekhem Went to Sojourn in Moav — Both Her Sons Died — Your People Shall Be My People — Ruth Clung to Her — They Came to Beit-Lekhem at the Beginning of the Barley Harvest

1 In the days when the judges ruled there was a famine in the land, and a man of Beit-Lekhem in Yehudah went to sojourn in the country of Moav, he and his wife and his two sons.

2 The name of the man was Elimelekh and the name of his wife Na'omi, and the names of his two sons were Makhlon and Kilyon. They were Efratites from Beit-Lekhem in Yehudah. They went into the country of Moav and remained there.

3 But Elimelekh, the husband of Na'omi, died, and she was left with her two sons.

4 These took Moabite wives. The name of the one was Orpah and the name of the other Ruth. They lived there about ten years,

5 And both Makhlon and Kilyon died, so that the woman was left without her two sons and her husband.

6 Then she arose with her daughters-in-law to return from the country of Moav, for she had heard in the fields of Moav that YHWH had visited his people and given them food.

7 So she set out from the place where she was with her two daughters-in-law, and they went on the way to return to the land of Yehudah.

8 But Na'omi said to her two daughters-in-law, Go, return each of you to her mother's house. May YHWH deal kindly with you, as you have dealt with the dead and with me.

9 YHWH grant that you may find rest, each of you in the house of her husband. Then she kissed them, and they lifted up their voices and wept.

10 And they said to her, No, we will return with you to your people.

11 But Na'omi said, Turn back, my daughters. Why will you go with me? Have I yet sons in my womb that they may become your husbands?

12 Turn back, my daughters. Go, for I am too old to have a husband. If I should say I have hope, even if I should have a husband this night and should bear sons,

13 Would you therefore wait till they were grown? Would you therefore refrain from marrying? No, my daughters, for it is exceedingly bitter to me for your sake that the hand of YHWH has gone out against me.

14 Then they lifted up their voices and wept again. And Orpah kissed her mother-in-law, but Ruth clung to her.

15 And she said, See, your sister-in-law has gone back to her people and to her gods. Return after your sister-in-law.

16 But Ruth said, Do not urge me to leave you or to return from following you. For where you go I will go, and where you lodge I will lodge. Your people shall be my people, and your Creator my Creator.

17 Where you die I will die, and there will I be buried. May YHWH do so to me and more also if anything but death separates me from you.

18 And when Na'omi saw that she was determined to go with her, she said no more.

19 So the two of them went on until they came to Beit-Lekhem. And when they came to Beit-Lekhem, the whole town was stirred because of them. And the women said, Is this Na'omi?

20 She said to them, Do not call me Na'omi. Call me Mara, for the Almighty has dealt very bitterly with me.

21 I went away full, and YHWH has brought me back empty. Why call me Na'omi, when YHWH has testified against me and the Almighty has brought calamity upon me?

22 So Na'omi returned, and Ruth the Moabite her daughter-in-law with her, who returned from the country of Moav. And they came to Beit-Lekhem at the beginning of barley harvest.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] Ruth is not preserved in the Dead Sea Scrolls corpus in any substantial manuscript form among the four Qumran Ruth fragments. The book is preserved primarily through the Masoretic and Orit Ge'ez traditions. Ruth belongs to the covenant category of Covenant History — a short narrative account of covenant loyalty operating within the period of the covenant Shofetim. The covenant universalism of Ruth — a Moabite woman integrated into the covenant community through covenant hesed — is the covenant theological center of the book.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Ruth Chapter 1 in full. Verse 16-17 — where you go I will go, and where you lodge I will lodge. Your people shall be my people, and your Creator my Creator. Where you die I will die — is the covenant oath of covenant solidarity that Ruth makes to Na'omi. Ruth's covenant oath uses the covenant name — your Creator my Creator — making her covenant identification with Na'omi simultaneously a covenant identification with YHWH. The covenant community is entered through covenant loyalty to its covenant members.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Ruth as part of the Ketuvim — the covenant writings. Verse 20-21 — do not call me Na'omi. Call me Mara, for the Almighty has dealt very bitterly with me. I went away full, and YHWH has brought me back empty. Na'omi's covenant self-renaming is the covenant confession of covenant suffering: the name Na'omi means pleasant — Mara means bitter. The covenant woman who left full returns empty and renames herself accordingly.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14 — Orpah kissed her mother-in-law, but Ruth clung to her. The covenant distinction between two covenant acceptable responses to Na'omi's release: Orpah's covenant kiss and departure is presented without condemnation — Na'omi herself urged it. Ruth's covenant clinging — davak, the covenant bonding word of Bereshit 2:24 — is the covenant extraordinary response that the entire narrative will unfold. The covenant kiss is covenant appropriate farewell; the covenant clinging is covenant extraordinary loyalty.

RUTH — CHAPTER 2 Ruth Went to Glean in the Fields — Boaz Came From Beit-Lekhem — Whose Young Woman Is This? — Do Not Go to Glean in Another Field — She Is a Moabite — May YHWH Repay You

1 Now Na'omi had a relative of her husband's, a worthy man of the clan of Elimelekh, whose name was Boaz.

2 And Ruth the Moabite said to Na'omi, Let me go to the field and glean among the ears of grain after him in whose sight I shall find favor. And she said to her, Go, my daughter.

3 So she set out and went and gleaned in the field after the reapers, and she happened to come to the part of the field belonging to Boaz, who was of the clan of Elimelekh.

4 And behold, Boaz came from Beit-Lekhem. And he said to the reapers, YHWH be with you. And they answered, YHWH bless you.

5 Then Boaz said to his young man who was in charge of the reapers, Whose young woman is this?

6 And the young man who was in charge of the reapers answered, She is the young Moabite woman, who came back with Na'omi from the country of Moav.

7 She said, Please let me glean and gather among the sheaves after the reapers. So she came, and she has continued from early morning until now, except for a short rest.

8 Then Boaz said to Ruth, Now, listen, my daughter, do not go to glean in another field or leave this one, but keep close to my young women.

9 Let your eyes be on the field that they are reaping, and go after them. Have I not charged the young men not to touch you? And when you are thirsty, go to the vessels and drink what the young men have drawn.

10 Then she fell on her face, bowing to the ground, and said to him, Why have I found favor in your eyes, that you should take notice of me, since I am a foreigner?

11 But Boaz answered her, All that you have done for your mother-in-law since the death of your husband has been fully told to me, and how you left your father and mother and your native land and came to a people that you did not know before.

12 YHWH repay you for what you have done, and a full reward be given you by YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, under whose wings you have come to take refuge.

13 Then she said, I have found favor in your eyes, my lord, for you have comforted me and spoken kindly to your servant, though I am not one of your servants.

14 And at mealtime Boaz said to her, Come here and eat some bread and dip your morsel in the wine. So she sat beside the reapers, and he passed to her roasted grain. And she ate until she was satisfied, and she had some left over.

15 When she rose to glean, Boaz instructed his young men, saying, Let her glean even among the sheaves, and do not reproach her.

16 And also pull out some from the bundles for her and leave it for her to glean, and do not rebuke her.

17 So she gleaned in the field until evening. Then she beat out what she had gleaned, and it was about an ephah of barley.

18 And she took it up and went into the city. Her mother-in-law saw what she had gleaned. She also brought out and gave her what food she had left over after being satisfied.

19 And her mother-in-law said to her, Where did you glean today? And where have you worked? Blessed be the man who took notice of you. So she told her mother-in-law with whom she had worked and said, The man's name with whom I worked today is Boaz.

20 And Na'omi said to her daughter-in-law, May he be blessed by YHWH, whose kindness has not forsaken the living or the dead. Na'omi also said to her, The man is a close relative of ours, one of our redeemers.

21 And Ruth the Moabite said, Besides, he said to me, You shall keep close to my young men until they have finished all my harvest.

22 And Na'omi said to Ruth, her daughter-in-law, It is good, my daughter, that you go out with his young women, lest in another field you be assaulted.

23 So she kept close to the young women of Boaz, gleaning until the end of the barley and wheat harvests. And she lived with her mother-in-law.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant agricultural law of gleaning — Vayikra 19:9-10, 23:22 — is the covenant legal framework that makes Ruth's covenant access to Boaz's field possible. The covenant law of gleaning was designed precisely for the covenant sojourner and the covenant poor. Ruth's covenant use of the covenant gleaning right is the covenant law functioning as the covenant intended: providing covenant access to the covenant community's covenant resources for the covenant vulnerable.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verse 12 — YHWH repay you for what you have done, and a full reward be given you by YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, under whose wings you have come to take refuge. Boaz's covenant blessing of Ruth uses the covenant image of the wings of YHWH — the covenant sheltering wings beneath which the covenant stranger finds covenant refuge. The covenant image will be echoed by Ruth herself in Chapter 3.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verse 20 — the man is a close relative of ours, one of our redeemers. Na'omi's covenant identification of Boaz as a covenant go'el — redeemer — is the covenant legal pivot on which the entire narrative turns: the covenant law of the go'el — Vayikra 25:25 — requires the nearest covenant male relative to redeem covenant property and covenant continue the covenant family line of a dead covenant kinsman.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 3 — she happened to come to the part of the field belonging to Boaz. The covenant narrative's covenant theological understatement: the Hebrew — vayiker mikhreha — literally means her happening happened. The covenant narrative acknowledges the covenant apparent randomness of Ruth's arrival in Boaz's field while the covenant reader understands it as covenant divine covenant providence. The covenant book of Ruth consistently operates in the mode of covenant hiddenness: YHWH's covenant direction is present but never covenant announced.

RUTH — CHAPTER 3 Wash and Anoint Yourself — Go Down to the Threshing Floor — Spread Your Wings Over Your Servant — He Will Not Rest Until He Has Settled the Matter — Lie Down Until the Morning

1 Then Na'omi her mother-in-law said to her, My daughter, should I not seek rest for you, that it may be well with you?

2 Is not Boaz our relative, with whose young women you were? See, he is winnowing barley tonight at the threshing floor.

3 Wash therefore and anoint yourself, and put on your cloak and go down to the threshing floor, but do not make yourself known to the man until he has finished eating and drinking.

4 But when he lies down, observe the place where he lies. Then go and uncover his feet and lie down, and he will tell you what to do.

5 And she replied, All that you say I will do.

6 So she went down to the threshing floor and did just as her mother-in-law had commanded her.

7 And when Boaz had eaten and drunk, and his heart was merry, he went to lie down at the end of the heap of grain. Then she came softly and uncovered his feet and lay down.

8 At midnight the man was startled and turned over, and behold, a woman lay at his feet.

9 He said, Who are you? And she answered, I am Ruth, your servant. Spread your wings over your servant, for you are a redeemer.

10 And he said, May you be blessed by YHWH, my daughter. You have made this last kindness greater than the first in that you have not gone after young men, whether poor or rich.

11 And now, my daughter, do not fear. I will do for you all that you ask, for all my fellow townsmen know that you are a worthy woman.

12 And now it is true that I am a redeemer. Yet there is a redeemer nearer than I.

13 Remain tonight, and in the morning, if he will redeem you, good. Let him do it. But if he is not willing to redeem you, then, as YHWH lives, I will redeem you. Lie down until the morning.

14 So she lay at his feet until the morning, but arose before one could recognize another. And he said, Let it not be known that the woman came to the threshing floor.

15 And he said, Bring the garment you are wearing and hold it out. So she held it, and he measured out six measures of barley and put it on her. Then she went into the city.

16 And when she came to her mother-in-law, she said, How did you fare, my daughter? Then she told her all that the man had done for her,

17 Saying, These six measures of barley he gave to me, for he said, You must not go back empty-handed to your mother-in-law.

18 She replied, Wait, my daughter, until you learn how the matter turns out, for the man will not rest but will settle the matter today.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant threshing floor scene of Chapter 3 is the covenant pivot of the book. Ruth's covenant request — spread your wings over your servant, for you are a redeemer — deliberately echoes Boaz's own covenant blessing of Chapter 2:12 — under whose wings you have come to take refuge. Ruth asks Boaz to be the answer to his own covenant prayer: the covenant wings of YHWH are requested to operate through the covenant wings of the covenant redeemer.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full. Verse 9 — spread your wings over your servant, for you are a redeemer. Ruth's covenant request is simultaneously a covenant marriage proposal and a covenant legal claim: she asks Boaz to exercise the covenant go'el obligation by marrying her to raise up the name of the dead — covenant levirate within the covenant go'el framework. The covenant wings of protection and the covenant legal obligation of redemption are united in one covenant request.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 3 in full. Verse 10 — you have made this last kindness greater than the first in that you have not gone after young men, whether poor or rich. Boaz's covenant recognition of the covenant order of Ruth's covenant hesed: the first kindness was covenant loyalty to Na'omi — covenant personal covenant fidelity. The second kindness — the covenant threshing floor — is covenant loyalty to the covenant family name of Elimelekh through the covenant go'el institution rather than seeking covenant personal romantic covenant advantage.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 11 — all my fellow townsmen know that you are a worthy woman. The Hebrew — eshet khayil — is the covenant phrase used in Mishlei 31:10 for the covenant excellent wife. Boaz applies to Ruth the same covenant standard used for the covenant ideal covenant woman in the covenant wisdom tradition. The covenant Moabite foreigner has achieved the covenant reputation of the covenant ideal covenant woman within the covenant community — before the covenant marriage that will formally integrate her.

RUTH — CHAPTER 4 Boaz Went to the Gate — The Redeemer Said I Cannot Redeem — He Drew Off His Sandal — Boaz Acquired Ruth — YHWH Made Her Conceive — A Son Is Born to Na'omi — The Genealogy of Dawid

1 Now Boaz had gone up to the gate and sat down there. And behold, the redeemer, of whom Boaz had spoken, came by. So Boaz said, Turn aside, friend, sit down here. And he turned aside and sat down.

2 And he took ten men of the elders of the city and said, Sit down here. So they sat down.

3 Then he said to the redeemer, Na'omi, who has come back from the country of Moav, is selling the parcel of land that belonged to our relative Elimelekh.

4 So I thought I would tell you of it and say, Buy it in the presence of those sitting here and in the presence of the elders of my people. If you will redeem it, redeem it. But if you will not, tell me, that I may know, for there is no one besides you to redeem it, and I come after you. And he said, I will redeem it.

5 Then Boaz said, The day you buy the field from the hand of Na'omi, you also acquire Ruth the Moabite, the widow of the dead, in order to perpetuate the name of the dead in his inheritance.

6 Then the redeemer said, I cannot redeem it for myself, lest I impair my own inheritance. Take my right of redemption yourself, for I cannot redeem it.

7 Now this was the custom in former times in Yisra'EL concerning redeeming and exchanging: to confirm a transaction, the one drew off his sandal and gave it to the other, and this was the manner of attesting in Yisra'EL.

8 So when the redeemer said to Boaz, Buy it for yourself, he drew off his sandal.

9 Then Boaz said to the elders and all the people, You are witnesses this day that I have bought from the hand of Na'omi all that belonged to Elimelekh and all that belonged to Khilyon and to Makhlon.

10 Also Ruth the Moabite, the widow of Makhlon, I have acquired to be my wife, to perpetuate the name of the dead in his inheritance, that the name of the dead may not be cut off from among his brothers and from the gate of his native place. You are witnesses this day.

11 Then all the people who were at the gate and the elders said, We are witnesses. May YHWH make the woman who is coming into your house like Rakhel and Le'ah, who together built up the house of Yisra'EL. May you act worthily in Efratah and be renowned in Beit-Lekhem,

12 And may your house be like the house of Peretz, whom Tamar bore to Yehudah, because of the offspring that YHWH will give you by this young woman.

13 So Boaz took Ruth, and she became his wife. And he went in to her, and YHWH gave her conception, and she bore a son.

14 Then the women said to Na'omi, Blessed be YHWH, who has not left you this day without a redeemer, and may his name be renowned in Yisra'EL.

15 He shall be to you a restorer of life and a nourisher of your old age, for your daughter-in-law who loves you, who is more to you than seven sons, has given birth to him.

16 Then Na'omi took the child and laid him on her lap and became his nurse.

17 And the women of the neighborhood gave him a name, saying, A son has been born to Na'omi. They named him Oved. He was the father of Yishai, the father of Dawid.

18 Now these are the generations of Peretz: Peretz fathered Khetzron,

19 Khetzron fathered Ram, Ram fathered Amminadav,

20 Amminadav fathered Nakhshon, Nakhshon fathered Salmon,

21 Salmon fathered Boaz, Boaz fathered Oved,

22 Oved fathered Yishai, and Yishai fathered Dawid.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant legal procedure of Chapter 4 — the gate assembly, the ten elders as witnesses, the sandal transfer — is the covenant judicial and commercial covenant institution of the covenant community in action. The covenant gate of the city is the covenant forum for covenant legal transactions. Boaz's covenant conduct of the covenant redemption procedure is covenant meticulous and covenant public: no covenant ambiguity, no covenant private covenant dealing, covenant full covenant community witness.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 4 in full. Verse 15 — your daughter-in-law who loves you, who is more to you than seven sons, has given birth to him. The covenant honor given to Ruth by the covenant women of Beit-Lekhem is the covenant climax of the covenant community's reception of the covenant Moabite: she is declared more valuable than seven covenant sons — the covenant number of covenant completeness and covenant blessing. The covenant foreigner who chose covenant loyalty has received covenant community covenant honor exceeding the covenant communal highest standard.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 4 in full. Verse 17 — a son has been born to Na'omi. The covenant community's covenant naming of the child belongs covenantally to Na'omi: the covenant emptiness with which she returned — Chapter 1:21 — is covenantally filled by the covenant son born to Ruth. The covenant son is simultaneously Ruth's biological son and Na'omi's covenant son through the covenant go'el institution. Oved — servant — is the covenant name that connects the covenant Moabite Ruth to the covenant Davidic dynasty through this covenant child.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verses 18-22 — the covenant genealogy from Peretz to Dawid. The covenant book that began with a covenant famine in the days of the covenant judges ends with the covenant genealogy of the covenant greatest king of Yisra'EL. The covenant Moabite Ruth — who declared your people shall be my people — is the covenant great-grandmother of Dawid. The covenant outsider who chose covenant loyalty to the covenant community becomes the covenant ancestral link in the covenant Davidic covenant line. The covenant book of Ruth is the covenant demonstration that covenant faithfulness — hesed — is the covenant defining covenant characteristic of the covenant community, regardless of covenant ethnic covenant origin.

THE BOOK OF RUTH — COMPLETE

Volume Twenty-One — The Ancient Covenant Record

𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

YOUR PEOPLE SHALL BE MY PEOPLE AND YOUR CREATOR MY CREATOR

Ruth 1:16
